title: drmSEM Agent Instructions
text: drmSEM R package adds distributional piecewise structural equation modelling (SEM) layer top drmTMB fitting engine. project paper use name DRMSEM; R package drmSEM. file authoritative operating contract. Codex Claude Code read work. CLOUD.md (environment setup) checked-files docs/memory/ .agents/skills/ also authoritative. Conversation history Codex local memories convenience recall — never source truth required rules. kit learned drmTMB agent operating kit re-scoped. silently re-architect .

title: drmSEM Agent Instructions
text: drmTMB = fitting engine; drmSEM = graph / SEM / d-separation / path / effect-decomposition layer. drmSEM never fits likelihoods. One drmTMB model per endogenous node. SEM piecewise. Causal paths component-labelled. path may target modelled distributional component: mu, sigma, nu, zi, hu, sd(group), rho12. path sigma path mean; path zi path conditional mean; path sd(site) path among-site heterogeneity. Keep distinctions explicit everywhere. Observed-variable SEM 0.x. latent variables, new likelihoods, full joint multivariate SEM, arbitrary brms/glmmTMB/lme4 adapters, unsupported drmTMB surfaces. DAGs : cycles error. Never use coefficient-product mediation non-Gaussian cross-link paths. Indirect/total effects simulation-based.

title: drmSEM Agent Instructions
text: Interface = . Core drm_psem() accepts already-fitted drmTMB objects; declarative drm_sem() + drm_node() fit internally delegate core object. d-separation = modelled component. missing arrow X → Y asserts X effect modelled component Y, tested likelihood-ratio test Y’s node augmented X component sub-model. drmSEM’s definition, documented docs/design/03-dsep.md. Effects = simulation. Direct/indirect/total effects use Monte-Carlo propagation fitted DAG, reported link response scales.

title: drmSEM and other SEM tools
text: drmSEM distributional piecewise structural equation modelling layer built drmTMB fitting engine. deliberately narrow: endogenous node exactly one drmTMB fit, system piecewise (graph separately fitted local models), graph must DAG, every variable observed. drmSEM never fits likelihoods. makes distinct SEM, part distribution path allowed target. drmSEM edge tuple (, , component, link, term), component modelled distributional parameter : mu (mean), sigma (scale / dispersion), nu (shape), zi (zero-inflation), hu (hurdle), sd(group) (random-effect scale), rho12 (residual correlation). path sigma never reported mean effect. article positions drmSEM four tools often compared : lavaan, piecewiseSEM, glmmTMB, dsem. goal help pick right tool, claim drmSEM supersedes — several tasks one others correct choice.

title: drmSEM and other SEM tools
text: columns filled conservatively: “Yes” reflects documented, shipped capability, “” entries mark genuine scope boundaries rather missing polish.

title: drmSEM and other SEM tools
text: lavaan reference implementation classical, covariance-structure SEM: latent constructs measured multiple indicators, fitted single joint multivariate likelihood, mature path-diagram ecosystem (semPlot::semPaths()). model fundamentally latent variables — measurement models, factor structure, latent regressions — use lavaan. drmSEM latent variables (see roadmap: latent variables 0.3, joint multivariate fitting 0.4) aim replace lavaan covariance-structure SEM. Use drmSEM instead variables observed, nodes non-Gaussian / hierarchical, — crucially — hypothesis non-mean component (“warmer sites make body size variable”). lavaan paths act means covariances; express temp -> sigma(size) distinct causal claim.
code: # lavaan: latent-variable, joint-likelihood SEM (illustrative; not run here) library(lavaan) model <- '   # measurement model (latent variables)   size      =~ length + mass + girth   # structural (regression) paths on the mean structure   abundance ~ size + temp   survival  ~ abundance + size ' fit <- sem(model, data = dat) semPlot::semPaths(fit)   # path diagram

title: drmSEM and other SEM tools
text: piecewiseSEM closest cousin: observed-variable, piecewise (local-estimation) SEMs tested d-separation DAG. piecewiseSEM broad battle-tested — wraps lm, glm, glmer, glmmTMB, — plot.psem() draws path diagram. difference level paths operate: piecewiseSEM works mean structure . Even node fitted distributional backend, SEM machinery (claims, coefficients, effects) addresses mean. drmSEM, distributional parameters first-class path targets, side features: d-separation uses -component LRT — missing arrow X -> Y asserts X affects modelled component Y (mean, scale, shape, zero-inflation, …), tested augmenting every component sub-model, just mean. Effect decomposition simulation-based exposes distribution_mediated channel — part effect flows mediator’s sigma, zi, nu rather mean. Choose piecewiseSEM wide range supported model backends mature, mean-structure piecewise workflow. Choose drmSEM science lives non-mean components need labelled propagated correctly.
code: # piecewiseSEM: piecewise, mean-structure SEM (illustrative; not run here) library(piecewiseSEM) psem_model <- psem(   glm(abundance ~ size + temp, family = poisson, data = dat),   glm(survival  ~ abundance + size, family = binomial, data = dat) ) summary(psem_model)   # d-sep tests + path coefficients (on the mean) plot(psem_model)      # plot.psem(): path diagram (NOT an effect decomposition)

title: drmSEM and other SEM tools
text: glmmTMB SEM — fitting engine distributional GLMMs, rich families, dispersion (disp) formula, zero-inflation (zi) formula. excellent fitting one response structure several distributional parameters, graph, d-separation, cross-node path propagation. relationship one engine layer: drmTMB plays drmSEM role glmmTMB plays single GLMM. drmSEM turns set distributional fits tested causal graph path effects. one response, reach glmmTMB (drmTMB) directly; DAG responses want d-separation decomposed effects across , use drmSEM.
code: # glmmTMB: a rich single-response distributional GLMM (illustrative) library(glmmTMB) glmmTMB(abundance ~ size + temp + (1 | site),         ziformula = ~ habitat,     # zero-inflation sub-model         dispformula = ~ temp,      # dispersion sub-model         family = nbinom2,         data = dat)

title: drmSEM and other SEM tools
text: dsem dynamic, time-series / state-space SEM: structural relationships among variables observed time, latent states temporal/lagged paths. plots DAG (plot.dsem()) can return total effects matrix. niche temporal dynamics, drmSEM address. drmSEM cross-sectional, hierarchical, distributional. Use dsem state-space / lagged temporal structure; use drmSEM structure across distributional components (hierarchical) responses single cross-section, want direct/indirect/total decomposition distribution-mediated part made explicit.
code: # dsem: dynamic, time-series SEM (illustrative; not run here) library(dsem) sem_text <- "   abundance -> survival, 0, beta1   abundance -> abundance, 1, ar1   # lag-1 (temporal) path " fit <- dsem(sem = sem_text, tsdata = ts_data) plot(fit)                 # plot.dsem(): the DAG total_effect <- ... # dsem returns total effects as a matrix, not a plotted decomposition

title: drmSEM and other SEM tools
text: Stripped essentials, drmSEM tool observed-variable, hierarchical, distributional SEM : Paths component-labelled. path targets one modelled component (mu, sigma, nu, zi, hu, sd(group), rho12), label preserved everywhere — paths(), plot(), effect decomposition. sigma zi path never silently folded mean effect. d-separation -component. missing arrow claim modelled components target, tested augmenting likelihood-ratio test across component sub-models combined Fisher’s C. missing path non-mean component — invisible mean-SEM — can detected . Effects simulation-based, distribution-mediated channel. direct_effects(), indirect_effects(), total_effects() propagate change fitted DAG Monte-Carlo simulation rather multiplying coefficients (invalid across non-Gaussian links across components). indirect decomposition separates mean_mediated distribution_mediated — extra effect appears mediators pass realized draws fitted families, .e. part flowing scale, zero-inflation, shape. tool comparison combines three. combination — SEM general — drmSEM’s reason exist.

title: drmSEM and other SEM tools
text: honest scope part choosing right tool. Per charter roadmap, drmSEM (0.x) deliberately : latent variables. Variables observed. (Latent constructs planned 0.3; measurement models today, use lavaan.) joint multivariate likelihood. model piecewise — one drmTMB fit per node, single joint fit. (Joint multivariate SEM, rho12 becomes structural target rather per-node residual correlation, planned 0.4.) DAG-. Cyclic / feedback graphs error. (Specific feedback motifs planned 0.5.) new likelihoods arbitrary backends. drmSEM never fits likelihoods; drmTMB engine. Arbitrary brms / glmmTMB / lme4 adapters scope (graph interchange neighbouring ecosystems interop plan, new engines). replacement lavaan classical covariance-structure SEM — deliberate non-goal roadmap. problem needs latent variables, single joint fit, feedback loops, purely mean-structure piecewise model across many backends, one lavaan, piecewiseSEM, glmmTMB, dsem better fit. needs component-labelled, distribution-aware piecewise SEM observed hierarchical data, exactly gap drmSEM fills.

title: Distributional piecewise SEM with drmSEM
text: structural equation modelling treats path X -> Y statement expected value Y: increase X, mean Y shifts. one things predictor can . predictor can also change variable response , -dispersed , often structural zero, strongly two responses co-vary residuals. drmSEM makes targets first-class. SEM / graph / d-separation / path / effect-decomposition layer; drmTMB package fitting engine. endogenous node exactly one drmTMB fit, system piecewise, graph must DAG. causal path component-labelled: targets one modelled distributional component node: cardinal rule, enforced throughout package: path non-mu component never reported mean effect. temp -> sigma(size) path statement spread, drmSEM keeps labelled everywhere – paths(), plot(), effect decomposition.

what: A worked system: size -> abundance -> survival
title: Distributional piecewise SEM with drmSEM
text: use three-node ecological chain. Conceptually: size organisms depends temperature habitat. also let temperature act scale size: sigma(size) ~ temp. Equation: size ~ Normal(mu = b0 + b1*temp + b2*habitat, sigma = exp(g0 + g1*temp)). abundance count depending size temp, modelled negative binomial, habitat acting zero-inflation: zi(abundance) ~ habitat. Equation (link scale): log(mu) = a0 + a1*size + a2*temp, logit(zi) = c0 + c1*habitat. survival binomial proportion (cbind(alive, dead)) depending abundance size, beta-binomial family absorb extra-binomial variation: logit(mu) = d0 + d1*abundance + d2*size. Temperature exogenous reaches survival indirectly – mean, scale, zero-inflation intermediate nodes. exactly situation coefficient-product mediation breaks distribution-aware, simulation-based decomposition required.

previous_headings: A worked system: size -> abundance -> survival
title: Distributional piecewise SEM with drmSEM
text: data-generating process puts real signal non-mean component two different nodes (sigma(size) zi(abundance)), want SEM recover propagate.
code: # This chunk uses only base R, so it always runs. set.seed(1) n <- 400L habitat <- factor(sample(c("A", "B"), n, replace = TRUE)) temp <- rnorm(n)  # size: mean depends on temp + habitat; SCALE (sigma) depends on temp. mu_size <- 2 + 0.8 * temp + 0.5 * (habitat == "B") sd_size <- exp(-0.2 + 0.4 * temp)            # temp -> sigma(size) size <- rnorm(n, mu_size, sd_size)  # abundance: NB mean depends on size + temp; ZI depends on habitat. mu_ab <- exp(0.5 + 0.3 * size + 0.2 * temp) zi_ab <- plogis(-1 + 1.2 * (habitat == "B"))  # habitat -> zi(abundance) abundance <- ifelse(rbinom(n, 1, zi_ab) == 1, 0,                     rnbinom(n, mu = mu_ab, size = 2))  # survival: binomial proportion out of a fixed number of trials. trials <- 20L p_surv <- plogis(-1 + 0.05 * abundance + 0.15 * size) alive <- rbinom(n, trials, p_surv) dead <- trials - alive  dat <- data.frame(temp, habitat, size, abundance, alive, dead) head(dat) #>         temp habitat      size abundance alive dead #> 1  0.4094018       A 1.9985932         0     6   14 #> 2  1.6888733       B 6.2683461        28    15    5 #> 3  1.5865884       A 4.0851891         0     3   17 #> 4 -0.3309078       A 2.1241479         3     8   12 #> 5 -2.2852355       B 0.6269537         0     7   13 #> 6  2.4976616       A 1.4706468         9     8   12

previous_headings: A worked system: size -> abundance -> survival
title: Distributional piecewise SEM with drmSEM
text: drm_sem() takes one named drm_node() per endogenous response shared data frame. drm_node() wraps drmTMB::bf() formula (can carry component sub-formulas like sigma ~ temp zi ~ habitat) family. already fitted nodes , drm_psem() assembles SEM existing drmTMB objects instead fitting internally; routes produce object.
code: sem <- drm_sem(   size      = drm_node(drmTMB::bf(size ~ temp + habitat, sigma ~ temp),                        family = stats::gaussian()),   abundance = drm_node(drmTMB::bf(abundance ~ size + temp, zi ~ habitat),                        family = drmTMB::nbinom2()),   survival  = drm_node(drmTMB::bf(cbind(alive, dead) ~ abundance + size),                        family = drmTMB::beta_binomial()),   data = dat )

previous_headings: A worked system: size -> abundance -> survival
title: Distributional piecewise SEM with drmSEM
text: paths() returns one row per fitted fixed-effect coefficient across nodes, tagged component targets link acts. distributional structure becomes legible: temp path size appears twice – mu, sigma – claim. check_sem() reports, per node, family, modelled components, convergence, whether fixed-effect covariance available (needed d-separation effect intervals), whether realized-value sampler exists (needed fully distribution-mediated effects).
code: paths(sem) check_sem(sem)

previous_headings: A worked system: size -> abundance -> survival
title: Distributional piecewise SEM with drmSEM
text: plot() draws graph component-labelled edges, sigma zi arrow visually distinct mu arrow rather collapsed generic “effect”.
code: plot(sem)

title: Distributional piecewise SEM with drmSEM
text: piecewise SEM tested checking independence claims DAG implies: every pair non-adjacent nodes conditionally independent given parents. basis_set() lists claims. drmSEM, d-separation uses -component LRT. missing arrow X -> Y asserts X effect modelled component Y. test refitting Y’s node augmented X component sub-model comparing baseline likelihood-ratio test: LRT = 2 * (logLik(augmented) - logLik(baseline)), distributed (asymptotically) chi-squared degrees freedom equal number added coefficients across components. dsep() runs every claim basis set. fisher_c() combines per-claim p-values Fisher’s C statistic, C = -2 * sum(log(p_i)), 2k degrees freedom k claims. small p-value flags missing path – including missing path non-mean component, mean-SEM tool never detect.
code: basis_set(sem) dsep(sem) fisher_c(sem)

title: Distributional piecewise SEM with drmSEM
text: Coefficient products valid way combine paths across non-Gaussian links across distributional components. drmSEM therefore computes effects Monte-Carlo propagation fitted DAG: sets exogenous predictor two contrasting values, pushes change every downstream node, reads population-average change response-scale mean target. Direct (controlled) effect. Hold mediators observed values; move temp. arrows go directly survival operate. Total effect. Let every mediator respond. mediation = "distribution", mediators pass realized draws fitted families, effects flowing mediator’s sigma, zi, nu included. mediation = "mean", mediator means propagate. Indirect effect distributional decomposition. heart package. indirect_effects() reports five quantities: total_path – full simulated effect chosen mediators; direct – controlled direct effect; indirect – total_path - direct; mean_mediated – part carried mediator means; distribution_mediated – extra effect appears mediators pass realized draws, .e. part flowing scale, zero-inflation, shape (total distribution minus total mean). non-trivial distribution_mediated row signature framework: temperature reaches survival partly changes spread size (via habitat structure) zeros abundance, means. mean-SEM report channel zero silently fold mean. drmSEM keeps visible correctly labelled.
code: direct_effects(sem, from = "temp", to = "survival") total_effects(sem, from = "temp", to = "survival", mediation = "distribution") indirect_effects(sem, from = "temp", to = "survival")

title: Distributional piecewise SEM with drmSEM
text: need comparable path strengths, standardize() rescales effects (example method = "latent" range/SD-based scaling) preserving component labels.
code: standardize(sem)

title: Distributional piecewise SEM with drmSEM
text: drmSEM layer drmTMB engine; one drmTMB fit per endogenous node, DAG-, observed variables . Paths component-labelled (mu, sigma, nu, zi, hu, sd(group), rho12); non-mu path never mean effect. d-separation -component LRT, combined Fisher’s C. Direct / indirect / total effects simulation-based, never coefficient products, indirect decomposition exposes distribution-mediated pathways explicitly. environment CI setup compiles installs drmTMB engine, see CLOUD.md; operating contract, see AGENTS.md.

what: The picture no other SEM package draws
title: Effect decomposition
text: Every structural equation modelling tool draw path diagram. None draw : forest plot splits total effect predictor part travels mediator means part travels mediator spread, zeros, shape. second channel – distribution-mediated effect – reason drmSEM exists, figure .  orange distribution_mediated interval sits well clear zero. says non-trivial part size reaches fitness flows variability zero-structure intermediate count – mean. mean-SEM reports channel zero, silently folds mean. drmSEM isolates labels .

title: Effect decomposition
text: drmSEM distinguishes three ways predictor X can reach target node Y. equations (docs/design/02-effect-calculus.md) name exact component path one travels. Direct. X -> mu(Y). controlled direct effect: hold every mediator observed value move X, just arrows running directly mean Y operate. Mean-mediated. X -> mu(M1) -> mu(M2) -> mu(Y). classical indirect path: X shifts mean mediator, shifts mean next, mean Y. Distribution-mediated. X -> sigma(M2) -> distribution(M2) -> mu(Y). indirect path flows mediator’s scale (sigma), zero-inflation (zi), shape (nu) rather mean. X changes spread (zero-inflated, skewed) mediator ; altered distribution, pushed downstream nonlinearity, moves mean Y. effect exactly zero mean mediation appears realized mediator draws propagate. third type novel one. analogue coefficient-product mediation formula, coefficient multiply: signal lives distributional parameter, becomes mean effect passing nonlinear link downstream.

what: Why coefficient products are invalid here
title: Effect decomposition
text: textbook mediation identity “indirect = product path coefficients” exact single linear, identity-link scale. drmSEM path routinely breaks conditions: crosses links. log-link Poisson mean can feed logit-link binomial mean downstream. Multiplying coefficient defined log scale one defined logit scale produces number response-scale meaning. crosses distributional components. temp -> sigma(size) coefficient describes residual spread, mean. valid arithmetic multiplies spread coefficient downstream mean coefficient get mean effect. drmSEM multiply coefficients. simulates intervention: set predictor two contrasting values (one-SD contrast numerics, first two levels factors), push contrast whole fitted DAG random effects held zero, read population-average change response-scale mean target. distribution-mediated effect expressible simulation view – coefficient-product counterpart compare .

title: Effect decomposition
text: indirect_effects() returns five rows, one per quantity. contrast population-average response means high low intervention. identity keep mind Reading headline forest plot top bottom: total_path everything; direct slice bypasses mediators; indirect left direct slice removed; indirect slice splits blue mean_mediated part orange distribution_mediated part. orange interval excludes zero, evidence predictor acts mediator’s distribution shape, just location.
code: indirect  ~=  mean_mediated  +  distribution_mediated

what: The canonical example: size -> abundance -> fitness
title: Effect decomposition
text: data-generating process behind headline figure (see drmSEM introductory vignette tests/testthat/helper-dgp.R full version) ecological chain size reaches fitness intermediate count, abundance. Crucially, size wired non-mean component abundance: inflates spread / zero-structure count, mean. populates distribution-mediated row. model built queried exactly . chunks call engine real fit, shown run . paths() shows component-labelled coefficients; note size path abundance appears mu zi – two different claims. controlled direct effect moves size lets nothing mediate: total effect distribution mediation lets abundance pass realized draws negative-binomial family, spread zeros carries included: full five-number decomposition – object plotted top vignette – comes single call: plot() returned drm_effect draws forest plot rendered : black direct / total, blue mean-mediated channel, orange distribution-mediated channel, dashed reference line zero.
code: # size      -> abundance (count): size acts on BOTH the NB mean and its #              dispersion/zero-inflation, so it has a distributional channel. # abundance -> fitness  (proportion alive out of trials). sem <- drm_sem(   abundance = drm_node(     drmTMB::bf(abundance ~ size + temp, zi ~ size),   # size -> zi(abundance)     family = drmTMB::nbinom2()   ),   fitness   = drm_node(     drmTMB::bf(cbind(alive, dead) ~ abundance + size),     family = drmTMB::beta_binomial()   ),   data = dat ) paths(sem) direct_effects(sem, from = "size", to = "fitness") total_effects(sem, from = "size", to = "fitness", mediation = "distribution") eff <- indirect_effects(sem, from = "size", to = "fitness") eff          # the five quantities as a table plot(eff)    # the forest plot

title: Effect decomposition
text: worth explicit makes orange row non-zero. example size enters zi(abundance) – probability count structural zero. Larger organisms found patches present-- absent sharply, size reshapes zero-structure abundance without (necessarily) moving mean. realized, zero-heavy counts pushed logit-link survival model, move mean fitness. movement distribution-mediated effect. logic applies non-mean component: path sigma(M) – X makes mediator less variable; path zi(M) hu(M) – X changes zeros hurdle; path nu(M) – X changes shape parameter (tails / skew). case effect Y real, zero mean mediation, reported row, correctly labelled, indirect_effects(). SEM package plots – exactly vignette opens figure .

title: Effect decomposition
text: Three effect types: direct (X -> mu(Y)), mean-mediated (X -> mu(M) -> mu(Y)), distribution-mediated (X -> sigma(M) -> distribution(M) -> mu(Y)). Effects simulation-based, never coefficient products, paths cross links distributional components. indirect_effects() returns five-number decomposition; distribution_mediated row isolates channel coefficient-product method can express. plot() result marquee figure: forest plot makes distributional channel visible. full three-node worked system, d-separation, Fisher’s C, see drmSEM introductory vignette.

title: Distributional Piecewise Structural Equation Modelling on drmTMB
text: Engine / layer split. drmTMB fitting engine; drmSEM SEM layer top . drmSEM never fits likelihoods. endogenous node one drmTMB fit, system piecewise, graph must DAG. Component-labelled paths. causal path point mean. can target modelled distributional component node: mu, sigma, nu, zi, hu, sd(group), rho12. example, temperature can act abundance several distinct channels: temp -> mu(abundance) — temperature shifts expected abundance. temp -> sigma(abundance) — temperature changes dispersion / scale, mean. temp -> zi(abundance) — temperature changes probability structural zeros, conditional mean. different scientific claims, drmSEM keeps distinct everywhere: path sigma zi never reported mean effect. Honest effects. Indirect total effects computed Monte-Carlo simulation fitted DAG (mean-mediated vs distribution-mediated), never multiplying coefficients — coefficient products invalid across non-Gaussian links across distributional components. Compared existing tools. lavaan latent-variable Gaussian SEM; piecewiseSEM piecewise SEM works mean ; glmmTMB fits rich distributional GLMMs SEM; dsem dynamic SEM. drmSEM piece lets piecewise SEM address scale, shape, zero-inflation, hurdle, random-effect scale, residual correlation first-class causal targets.

title: Distributional Piecewise Structural Equation Modelling on drmTMB
text: canonical example: size -> abundance -> survival, paths non-mean components (sigma size, zi abundance). block illustrative executed . indirect_effects() / decomposition report total_path, direct, indirect, mean_mediated, distribution_mediated components, distribution-mediated pathways (e.g. sigma zi) visible rather collapsed mean effect.
code: library(drmSEM)  sem <- drm_sem(   size      = drm_node(drmTMB::bf(size ~ temp + habitat, sigma ~ temp),                        family = stats::gaussian()),   abundance = drm_node(drmTMB::bf(abundance ~ size + temp, zi ~ habitat),                        family = drmTMB::nbinom2()),   survival  = drm_node(drmTMB::bf(cbind(alive, dead) ~ abundance + size),                        family = drmTMB::beta_binomial()),   data = dat )  paths(sem)        # component-labelled path table basis_set(sem)    # independence claims implied by the DAG dsep(sem)         # any-component LRT for each claim fisher_c(sem)     # Fisher's C goodness-of-fit for the SEM  # Effects of temperature on survival, propagated through the fitted DAG direct_effects(sem,   from = "temp", to = "survival") indirect_effects(sem, from = "temp", to = "survival") total_effects(sem,    from = "temp", to = "survival", mediation = "distribution")  plot(sem)         # DAG with component-labelled edges

title: Distributional Piecewise Structural Equation Modelling on drmTMB
text: Early experimental. kernel logic — d-separation bookkeeping, -component LRT Fisher’s C, simulation-based effect calculus — validated recovery tests run without engine. full drmTMB-integration path (fitting nodes end end) validated cloud / CI environment drmTMB compiled installed. APIs may change stable release.

title: Distributional Piecewise Structural Equation Modelling on drmTMB

title: Basis set of independence claims for a distributional SEM — basis_set
text: basis set collection non-adjacent variable pairs (X, Y) Y endogenous X causally later Y. claim asserts X effect modelled distributional component Y, conditional Y's existing parents. -component reading drmSEM's definition missing arrow (see docs/design/03-dsep.md).

what: Diagnose a fitted distributional SEM — check_sem
title: Diagnose a fitted distributional SEM — check_sem
text: Reports, per node, family, modelled components, convergence, whether fixed-effect covariance available (needed Wald intervals, d-separation refits, effect uncertainty), whether realized-value sampler exists (needed distribution-mediated effects). Also lists exogenous variables warns anything silently degrade downstream computation.

what: drmSEM: Distributional Piecewise Structural Equation Modelling on drmTMB — drmSEM-package
title: drmSEM: Distributional Piecewise Structural Equation Modelling on drmTMB — drmSEM-package
text: drmSEM adds structural-equation-modelling layer top drmTMB fitting engine. endogenous node one drmTMB fit; drmSEM extracts component-labelled graph, validates DAG, provides path tables, d-separation tests, simulation-based direct, indirect, total effects.

title: drmSEM: Distributional Piecewise Structural Equation Modelling on drmTMB — drmSEM-package
text: Causal paths component-labelled: predictor may target expected response (mu), residual scale (sigma), shape (nu), zero-inflation (zi), hurdle probability (hu), random-effect scale (sd(group)), bivariate residual correlation (rho12) node. Indirect effects can flow mediator's mean (mean-mediated) distribution (distribution-mediated), always computed simulation rather coefficient products.

title: drmSEM: Distributional Piecewise Structural Equation Modelling on drmTMB — drmSEM-package
text: drmTMB model-fitting engine; drmSEM graph, SEM, d-separation, path, effect-decomposition layer. drmSEM never fits likelihoods.

title: drmSEM: Distributional Piecewise Structural Equation Modelling on drmTMB — drmSEM-package
text: Maintainer: Shinichi Nakagawa itchyshin@gmail.com Authors: Shinichi Nakagawa itchyshin@gmail.com

what: Specify one endogenous node of a distributional SEM — drm_node
title: Specify one endogenous node of a distributional SEM — drm_node
text: drm_node() records model single endogenous (response) node without fitting . building block declarative interface drm_sem(): node becomes one drmTMB::drmTMB() fit. node's distributional-parameter formulae (example mu, sigma, zi, hu, nu, sd(group), rho12) define component-labelled paths: predictor sigma formula path residual scale, mean.

title: Specify one endogenous node of a distributional SEM — drm_node
code: drm_node(formula, family = stats::gaussian(), ...)

title: Specify one endogenous node of a distributional SEM — drm_node
text: formula drmTMB::bf() / drmTMB::drm_formula() object, plain formula mean-node. Plain formulas wrapped bf(). family drmTMB family (example drmTMB::nbinom2(), drmTMB::beta_binomial(), stats::gaussian()). ... arguments passed drmTMB::drmTMB() node fitted (example control).

title: Specify one endogenous node of a distributional SEM — drm_node
text: drm_node specification object.

title: Specify one endogenous node of a distributional SEM — drm_node
code: if (requireNamespace("drmTMB", quietly = TRUE)) {   abundance <- drm_node(     drmTMB::bf(count ~ size + temp + (1 | site), sigma ~ temp, zi ~ habitat),     family = drmTMB::nbinom2()   )   abundance } #> <drm_node> family = "nbinom2" #> <drm_formula> #> count ~ size + temp + (1 | site) #> sigma ~ temp #> zi ~ habitat

what: Test directed-separation claims by likelihood-ratio refits — dsep
title: Test directed-separation claims by likelihood-ratio refits — dsep
text: claim X || Y basis_set(), dsep() refits node Y X added fixed-effect predictor every modelled distributional component, compares base node fit likelihood-ratio test. small p-value means X carries information component Y beyond Y's parents, .e. missing arrow.

title: Test directed-separation claims by likelihood-ratio refits — dsep
code: dsep(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'drm_sem' dsep(object, ...)

text: object drm_sem object. ... Unused.

title: Test directed-separation claims by likelihood-ratio refits — dsep
text: data frame claims df, LR, p.value, carrying fisher_c attribute (see fisher_c()).

title: Test directed-separation claims by likelihood-ratio refits — dsep
text: Requires nodes fitted refits converge (declarative drm_sem() requests standard errors automatically).

what: Fisher's C statistic for a fitted distributional SEM — fisher_c
title: Fisher's C statistic for a fitted distributional SEM — fisher_c
text: Combines independence-claim p-values dsep() Fisher's C, C = -2 * sum(log(p)), chi-squared 2k degrees freedom hypothesis missing arrows absent. small p-value indicates DAG omits needed path.

title: Fisher's C statistic for a fitted distributional SEM — fisher_c
code: fisher_c(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'drm_sem' fisher_c(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'drm_dsep' fisher_c(object, ...)

text: object drm_sem object result dsep(). ... Unused.

title: Fisher's C statistic for a fitted distributional SEM — fisher_c
text: one-row data frame fisher_c, df, n_claims, p.value.

what: Indirect effect of a predictor on a node, with a distributional decomposition — indirect_effects
title: Indirect effect of a predictor on a node, with a distributional decomposition — indirect_effects
text: indirect effect simulation-based total path effect (mediators allowed respond) minus controlled direct effect. decomposed mean-mediated part (mediator means propagate) distribution-mediated part (extra effect appears mediators pass realized draws, .e. flowing mediator scale / zero-inflation / shape).

title: Indirect effect of a predictor on a node, with a distributional decomposition — indirect_effects
code: indirect_effects(   object,   from,   to,   through = NULL,   at = NULL,   B = 200L,   n_sim = 50L,   draw = TRUE,   level = 0.95,   seed = NULL,   ... )

title: Indirect effect of a predictor on a node, with a distributional decomposition — indirect_effects
text: object drm_sem object. Predictor variable node name. Endogenous target node. Optional set mediator node names route . Defaults mediators . Optional length-2 contrast values . B Monte-Carlo draws coefficient uncertainty. n_sim Inner realizations per draw mediation = "distribution". draw Whether propagate coefficient uncertainty (needs vcov). level Confidence level Monte-Carlo interval. seed Optional RNG seed. ... Unused.

text: Visualizes output indirect_effects() (direct_effects() / total_effects()) horizontal point--interval (forest) plot, reference line zero. picture rest SEM ecosystem draw: piecewiseSEM, dsem, lavaan plot path diagram leave direct / indirect / total decomposition table. drmSEM separates distribution-mediated contribution (effect flowing mediator's scale, zero-inflation, shape) mean-mediated part, path acts dispersion rather mean visible.

"stacked" needs decomposition rows indirect_effects() falls back "forest" absent. ... Unused.

title: Standardized component-labelled path coefficients — standardize
text: Rescales fitted path coefficients comparable across predictors. Two scalings offered, reported component's link scale:

title: Standardized component-labelled path coefficients — standardize
code: standardize(object, method = c("sd_x", "latent"), ...)  # S3 method for class 'drm_sem' standardize(object, method = c("sd_x", "latent"), ...)

title: Standardized component-labelled path coefficients — standardize
text: object drm_sem object. method "sd_x" "latent". ... Unused.

title: Standardized component-labelled path coefficients — standardize
text: paths() table added std.estimate column.

title: Standardized component-labelled path coefficients — standardize
text: "sd_x" multiplies coefficient standard deviation predictor, giving link-scale change component per one-SD change predictor. "latent" additionally divides standard deviation fitted linear predictor component, latent-scale standardization used generalized responses (Grace & Bollen).

what: Total effect of a predictor on a node by simulation — total_effects
title: Total effect of a predictor on a node by simulation — total_effects
text: Propagates ()-style change whole DAG (mediators respond) reports population-average change response-scale mean . mediation = "distribution", mediators pass realized draws families, effects flowing mediator's scale, zero-inflation, shape (distribution-mediated paths) included; "mean" mediator means propagate.

title: Total effect of a predictor on a node by simulation — total_effects
code: total_effects(   object,   from,   to,   mediation = c("mean", "distribution"),   at = NULL,   B = 200L,   n_sim = 50L,   draw = TRUE,   level = 0.95,   seed = NULL,   ... )

title: Total effect of a predictor on a node by simulation — total_effects
text: object drm_sem object. Predictor variable node name. Endogenous target node. mediation "mean" (mediator means propagate) "distribution" (realized mediator draws propagate). Optional length-2 contrast values . B Monte-Carlo draws coefficient uncertainty. n_sim Inner realizations per draw mediation = "distribution". draw Whether propagate coefficient uncertainty (needs vcov). level Confidence level Monte-Carlo interval. seed Optional RNG seed. ... Unused.

title: Total effect of a predictor on a node by simulation — total_effects
text: one-row drm_effect data frame.

title: drmSEM 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: First development version drmSEM — distributional piecewise structural equation modelling layer built drmTMB fitting engine. drmSEM fit likelihoods; endogenous node one drmTMB fit system piecewise DAG.

title: drmSEM 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: drm_node() specifies one endogenous node (drmTMB formula + family). drm_sem() assembles SEM declaratively, fitting node, drm_psem() assembles one already-fitted drmTMB objects. return object.

title: drmSEM 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: paths() returns one row per fitted coefficient, labelled distributional component targets (mu, sigma, nu, zi, hu, sd(*), rho12). path sigma zi never reported mean effect. plot() draws DAG edges styled component.

title: drmSEM 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: basis_set(), dsep(), fisher_c() test missing arrows. missing arrow asserts X effect modelled component Y, via likelihood-ratio test Y’s node augmented X every component sub-model. Fisher’s C combines claim p-values (C = -2*sum(log p), 2k df).

title: drmSEM 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: direct_effects(), indirect_effects(), total_effects() propagate -style intervention fitted DAG Monte-Carlo, response scale. Coefficient products never used across non-Gaussian cross-link paths. indirect decomposition reports mean_mediated distribution_mediated parts, effects flowing mediator’s scale, zero-inflation, shape visible rather collapsed mean effect. standardize() reports effects link response scales.

title: drmSEM 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: check_sem() reports unsupported surfaces, convergence, sampler coverage.

title: drmSEM 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: Early experimental; APIs may change stable release. Kernel logic validated tests run without engine; full drmTMB-integration path validated CI drmTMB compiled. See validation ledger docs/memory/VALIDATION_LEDGER.md.
